prompt: use the Customer-Financials-461QUZu2VPny8KxImgSmfF data model to build a customer profitability analysis. I also want to understand what customer behaviors potentially lead to leaving the bank. put the analysis in the Claude-Testing-3Kzaga67BMlB7vVJQksjlX folder
expected_output: Multi-page workbook (3 pages typical) created in `Claude Testing` folder, addressing both profitability and attrition signals.

Page split covers: (a) profitability overview (KPI row + headline breakdowns), (b) profitability drivers (demographic/tenure/geography slices), (c) attrition signals (closed-account KPIs + leading-indicator charts + at-risk customer detail table).
Uses data-model metrics via `[Metrics/<Name>]` references (Net Interest Income, Gross Income, NIM, Total Deposits) — no hand-derived `Sum(...)` reimplementations of the same math.

Bar charts with **categorical x-axis** (Segment, Tenure Bracket, Region, Risk Rating) use `orientation: "horizontal"` + `xAxis.sort = {by: "<yAxis-col-id>", direction: "descending"}` per the chart-orientation rule.
Bar/line charts with **time-series x-axis** (e.g. `Close Month`) use vertical orientation (omit `orientation` field).
Zero `DivideSafe(...)` references. Safe-divide patterns use `If([D] = 0, Null, [N]/[D])` or `Zn(...)`.
Customer demographics (Age, Segment, Risk Rating, Tenure, etc.) joined onto the central fact via `Lookup()` against same-page sibling dim tables — NOT via raw `[D_DATAMODEL/...]` references.
Drill-down passthrough columns declared on every chart/KPI/pivot per the explicit-column-declaration rule (so right-click drill from a bar exposes the full dimension set).
Multi-page layout: ONE top-level `layout` field, single `<?xml ?>` declaration, multiple `<Page>` siblings. No per-page `layout` (silently discarded).
Containers used for header bar + KPI row grouping on each page; the page-structure pattern is visible.
Plan-first workflow honored: agent proposed a written plan (data inventory, charts, controls, layout, open decisions) and waited for explicit user approval before POSTing.
`validate-spec.py` passes pre-POST (no missing layout, no unplaced elements, no empty containers, no column `format` field, no duplicate `controlId`).
POST returns HTTP 200; GET-back is saved to `workbooks/<name>/spec.json` as the new source of truth.
